purpose: Audit DamageEngine.applyDamage callsites after Phase 10G wiring to determine which paths can truthfully supply attacker/sourceActor, targetActor, hit, critical, and timing context for DamageTimingRiderAdapter.
relationshipTo10G: Phase 10G wired DamageTimingRiderAdapter into DamageEngine. Phase 10H classifies and scopes the callsites so context is propagated only where it is truthful.
rules: Do not invent an attacker for manual or environmental damage.
Attack-resolution callsites should pass attacker/sourceActor, targetActor, weapon, hit, critical, and damage context where available.
Recurring, poison, collision, and manual damage should not trigger attacker feat damage riders unless they have a real source actor and represent a successful attack or rider-eligible damage event.
Advantageous Attack remains not implemented_correct until wrong-shape attack metadata is quarantined and a focused test proves damage-only behavior.

recommendedOptions: critical/isCritical from attack roll when available
damageType from weapon combat damage type
targetHasActedThisEncounter when exact combat state exists
callsites.phase10hFinding: This is the primary callsite for Advantageous Attack and other successful-hit damage riders. It should be the first code wiring target.
Manual GM damage should not trigger feat damage riders unless the GM explicitly supplies source context later.

callsites.callsite: PoisonEngine._resolvePoisonDamage / poison damage paths -> DamageEngine.applyDamage(targetActor, amount, ...)
recommendedOptions: skipDamageTimingRiders: true unless a poison-specific feat explicitly opts in
callsites.phase10hFinding: Poison has a source actor but is its own subsystem. It should preserve source context for logging but not trigger weapon hit damage riders by default.

recommendedOptions: sourceActor resolved from instance source actor when available
callsites.phase10hFinding: Recurring damage should preserve source metadata but should not re-trigger on-hit damage riders each tick.

recommendedOptions: skipDamageTimingRiders: true unless a collision-specific feat opts in
callsites.phase10hFinding: Vehicle collision damage has a source actor but is not a normal weapon hit. It should not trigger Advantageous Attack.

nextImplementationRecommendation.goal: Patch CombatEngine.resolveAttack to pass exact attack context into DamageEngine.applyDamage, and patch manual/subsystem callsites with skipDamageTimingRiders guards where appropriate.
minimumAcceptance: CombatEngine damage calls pass attacker/sourceActor, targetActor, weapon, hit/isHit, critical/isCritical, and damageType.
Manual damage app passes skipDamageTimingRiders: true.
Recurring/collision subsystem damage passes skipDamageTimingRiders: true unless explicitly opted in.
Audit proves Advantageous Attack can only modify damage in attack-context calls.
